spec_match.rationale: The demo correctly uses image(<color>) in both content (for generated avatars) and border-image (for edge accents).

interactive.rationale: Includes good interactive controls: oklch color switcher for avatars and a border-image layout override controller.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The parent blurb aligns perfectly with the content and border-image concepts shown.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers multiple distinct properties (content, border-image) and various edge configurations (top, bottom, left, fat left).

references.rationale: Includes the mandatory ChromeStatus entry reference link.

design_system.rationale: The avatar initials use light ivory text on top of light oklch backgrounds (62-70% lightness), which likely violates the WCAG AA contrast requirement.

openQuestions.title: Improve text contrast on avatar placeholders
openQuestions.detail: The avatar initials use `color: var(--bg-ivory)` (very light) on top of background colors with ~62-70% lightness (e.g., `oklch(70% 0.14 250)`). This likely violates the WCAG AA contrast ratio (4.5:1) hard rule. Suggest using `color: var(--text-black)` or selecting darker background colors (lower lightness in oklch) to ensure readability.

summary: A highly comprehensive demo that beautifully illustrates the flexibility of image() in content and border-image. It features strong interactivity, but has a minor contrast accessibility issue in the avatar initials overlay.
